relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Passover draws near.
Leaders seek removal while fearing the people.
Judas opens to the adversarial current.
Betrayal is negotiated with authorities.
Silver seals their joy in the plot.
Opportunity is sought away from the crowd.
The Passover day arrives.
Peter and John are sent to prepare.
They ask where preparation should happen.
A water-bearer marks the path to the house.
The Teacher asks for the guest room.
The furnished upper room is ready.
They find it as spoken and prepare.
The hour gathers him with the apostles.
He has deeply desired this Passover before suffering.
Fulfillment waits in the Divine reign.
The cup is given with thanks and shared.
New drinking waits for the coming reign.
Bread is blessed, broken, given, and remembered.
The cup becomes new covenant poured out.
The betrayer’s hand is at the table.
The Son of Humanity goes according to what is determined, yet betrayal bears woe.
The disciples wonder who could do this.
Even at the table, greatness is disputed.
Nation-power lords over and calls itself benefactor.
Among them, greatness becomes youthfulness and leadership service.
Jesus is among them as one serving.
They have remained with him in testing.
A reign is appointed to them.
Table and thrones belong to the coming reign.
Simon is warned of sifting.
Jesus prays that trust not fail and that return strengthen siblings.
Peter imagines himself ready for prison and death.
Jesus names the coming threefold denial.
The earlier mission lacked nothing.
Now the hour requires readiness and sober danger.
The written word about being counted with lawless ones is completed.
Two swords are enough for the misunderstanding to stop.
He goes to Olivet and the disciples follow.
Prayer is needed not to enter testing.
He withdraws, kneels, and prays.
The cup is surrendered into the Father’s will.
A textual tradition speaks of a heavenly messenger strengthening him.
A textual tradition speaks of agony and intense prayer.
He finds the disciples sleeping from grief.
They are called to rise and pray.
Judas comes before the crowd with a kiss.
The kiss becomes the sign of handing-over.
Those around him ask whether to strike.
A servant’s ear is cut.
Jesus heals even in the arresting moment.
He exposes their armed approach as unnecessary.
This is their hour and the authority of darkness.
Jesus is led away while Peter follows far off.
Peter sits in the courtyard firelight.
A servant girl recognizes him.
He denies knowing him.
A second recognition meets denial.
His Galilean identity is named.
The third denial meets the rooster.
The Lord turns and looks; memory opens.
Peter goes out and weeps bitterly.
The guards mock and strike Jesus.
Covered eyes become cruel mock-prophecy.
Many harmful words are spoken against him.
Day brings council gathering.
They ask whether he is Messiah, but trust is absent.
Their questioning cannot receive answer.
The Son of Humanity will sit at the right hand of power.
They ask Son of the Divine, and he answers in their own words.
They claim no further testimony is needed.
